title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 高脂血症（高胆固醇）的定义：血液中脂质水平过高，主要脂质类型为胆固醇和甘油三酯
text_en: If you have hyperlipedemia, commonly known as high cholesterol, you have high levels of lipids in your blood. Lipids are fats and fat-like substances. In this condition, the most common types of lipids are cholesterol and triglycerides.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 胆固醇的正常生理功能：肝脏合成胆固醇，用于构成细胞膜、皮肤合成维生素D、腺体合成激素、肝脏合成胆汁酸帮助消化脂肪
text_en: Your body needs cholesterol to function properly. It makes all the cholesterol you need, mostly in your liver. The cells in your body need cholesterol as part of their cell membrane. Your skin uses sunlight and cholesterol to make vitamin D. Certain glands, such as the testicles in men and the adrenal glands, use cholesterol to make important chemicals called hormones. And your liver uses cholesterol to make bile acids, which help to digest fat in the food

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 甘油三酯的功能与来源：为身体提供能量，来源于食物和肝脏，高脂食物、精制碳水化合物、高糖食物和酒精会升高甘油三酯
text_en: Your body uses triglycerides for energy. Triglycerides in your blood come from food and your liver. Foods high in triglycerides include fatty foods, refined carbohydrates, foods high in simple sugars, and alcohol. From your liver, your lipids are not able to move through your bloodstream without some adjustment.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 脂蛋白的形成与分类：肝脏将蛋白质包裹在脂质外形成脂蛋白以便在血液中运输，脂蛋白类型决定胆固醇的好坏（VLDL为坏胆固醇LDL的来源）
text_en: To enable their movement, your liver wraps certain proteins around the lipids. The resulting new molecule called a lipoprotein can move through your bloodstream and throughout your body to the cells that need it. Depending on which type of lipoprotein your cholesterol is part of, determines whether your cholesterol is good or bad. For example, your liver makes very low-density lipoproteins, or VLDLs, which are the source of bad cholesterol, or LDL.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: VLDL的代谢过程：VLDL富含胆固醇和甘油三酯，在血液中运输甘油三酯至细胞供能；多余的甘油三酯储存为体脂，VLDL失去甘油三酯后转变为LDL
text_en: These lipoproteins are stuffed with cholesterol and triglycerides. VLDL travels through your bloodstream, delivering triglycerides to your cells, which use them for energy. If your liver makes more VLDL than your body needs for energy, the VLDL stores the extra triglycerides as body fat.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: LDL（坏胆固醇）与动脉粥样硬化：LDL过多会在血管壁沉积形成脂质斑块，血管壁损伤更易促进斑块形成，逐渐使血管狭窄、血流减少
text_en: After losing its triglycerides, a VLDL becomes a low-density lipoprotein or LDL. LDL travels through your bloodstream, delivering cholesterol to the cells that need it. If your body makes too much LDL, it can build up in your blood. LDL can deposit in the walls of your blood vessels, causing a build-up of fatty material called plaque. Ligitage to vessel walls can make it easier for LDL to form plaques. Over time, this build-up can narrow the blood vessel and reduce blood flow. This is why LDL is called the bad cholesterol.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 斑块的临床后果：冠状动脉斑块导致冠心病和心脏病发作风险增加，颈动脉斑块减少脑部血流并增加中风风险
text_en: A common place this plaque can build up is in your coronary arteries, which are the blood vessels that feed your heart muscle. This build-up causes coronary artery disease and increases your risk of a heart attack. Plac in other arteries, such as the carotid arteries in your neck, can reduce blood flow to your brain and increase the risk of a stroke.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: HDL（好胆固醇）的保护作用：HDL含蛋白质多、脂质少，能从细胞和血管斑块中清除多余胆固醇，并将其运回肝脏排出体外（胆固醇逆转运）
text_en: Your liver also makes high-density lipoprotein or HDL, also known as the good cholesterol. HDL has more protein and very little cholesterol and triglycerides compared to LDL. HDL helps remove excess cholesterol from your cells and from plaque in your blood vessels. This is why HDL is called the good cholesterol. HDL returns the excess cholesterol to your liver, which removes it from your body.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 血脂检测方法：建议20岁以上每五年做一次空腹脂蛋白谱检测，需禁食9-12小时后抽血，检测总胆固醇、LDL、HDL和甘油三酯水平
text_en: If you are 20 years of age or older, the National Institutes of Health recommends you have a blood test every five years called a fasting lipoprotein profile. It measures your levels of total cholesterol, LDL, HDL and triglycerides. During this test, a blood sample will be taken from your arm or finger after you have not eaten for nine to twelve hours.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 血脂目标值：总胆固醇<200、HDL≥60、LDL<100、空腹甘油三酯<150（mg/dL）；具体目标因个人健康状况而异，建议咨询医生
text_en: The total cholesterol goal should be less than 200 mg per desoliter. For most people, an ideal HDL or good cholesterol should be 60 or higher. An ideal LDL or bad cholesterol should be less than 100. And fasting triglycerides should be less than 150. Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation. Ask your doctor what your lipid goals should be.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 饮食管理与脂肪类型：心脏健康饮食应多吃瘦肉蛋白和蔬果；饱和脂肪和反式脂肪升高LDL（来源于肉类、乳制品、加工食品、热带油脂），不饱和脂肪更健康（来源于鱼、坚果、植物油）
text_en: If your cholesterol levels are too high, it's important to eat a heart-healthy diet with lean sources of protein and plenty of fruits and vegetables. Also, the types of fats you eat can affect your cholesterol level. For example, saturated fats and trans fats tend to raise LDL cholesterol in your blood. These fats are usually solid at room temperature and are found in meat and dairy products, many processed foods and tropical oils such as coconut oil, palm oil and cocoa butter. Unsaturated fats, including polyunsaturated and mono-unsaturated fats, are healthier fats that are usually more liquid at room temperature. They are found in foods such as fish, nuts and vegetable oils.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 生活方式干预：戒烟和规律运动（如快走、跑步）有助于改善胆固醇水平；生活方式改变不足时医生可能开具药物
text_en: Other lifestyle decisions you can make to help your cholesterol are not smoking and getting regular exercise such as brisk walking or running. If lifestyle changes can reduce your cholesterol levels enough, your doctor may prescribe certain medications to reduce it.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 降脂药物治疗：他汀类（减少肝脏合成胆固醇）、烟酸（降LDL和甘油三酯、升HDL）、胆汁酸结合剂（促进肝脏消耗胆固醇补充胆汁）、贝特类（降甘油三酯、升HDL）、胆固醇吸收抑制剂（减少肠道吸收）
text_en: Statins are drugs that reduce the amount of cholesterol made in your liver. They affect LDL levels more than HDL or triglyceride levels. Niasin reduces LDL cholesterol and triglycerides and increases HDL cholesterol made in your liver. Bial acid binding resins are drugs that prevent the reuse of bile after helping to digest your food. As a result, your liver uses more cholesterol to replace the lost bile, which means less cholesterol in your bloodstream. Fibrates are mainly used to reduce triglyceride levels in your blood, but they can also raise HDL levels. Cholesterol absorption inhibitors reduce the amount of cholesterol absorbed by your intestines from the food you eat.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 颅脑解剖结构：头骨作为大脑的保护层，头骨内侧有三层脑膜（硬脑膜、蛛网膜、软脑膜），蛛网膜与软脑膜之间的蛛网膜下腔含有血管和脑脊液
text_en: Your skull is made of bone and serves as a hard protective covering for your brain. Just inside your skull, three layers of tissue called meninges surround your brain. The thick, outermost layer is the duromotor. The middle tissue layer is the arachnoid motor. And the innermost layer is the pia-motor. Between the arachnoid motor and the pia-motor is the subarachnoid space, which contains blood vessels and a clear fluid called cerebrospinal fluid.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 脑部血管与颅内压平衡：桥静脉连接脑表面与硬脑膜，脑动脉为大脑供血；正常脑功能需要血管内血液、脑脊液和脑组织之间的压力平衡，即正常颅内压
text_en: Blood vessels, called bridging veins, connect the surface of your brain with the duromotor. Other blood vessels, called cerebral arteries, bring blood to your brain. Inside your skull, normal brain function requires a delicate balance of pressure between the blood in your blood vessels, the cerebrospinal fluid that surrounds your brain, and your brain This is called normal intracranial pressure.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 颅内压升高的原因与后果：脑肿瘤、头部损伤、血管问题或脑脊髓感染可导致颅内压升高，压迫大脑引起肿胀或变形，可能导致严重脑损伤
text_en: Increased intracranial pressure may result from brain tumors, head injuries, problems with your blood vessels, or infections in your brain or spinal cord. These conditions put pressure on your brain and may cause it to swell or change shape inside your skull, which can lead to serious brain injury.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 开颅术的适应症：切除脑肿瘤、活检取样、清除血肿、引流脑脊液、排脓；缓解脑肿胀、止血、修复异常血管和颅骨骨折、修复受损脑膜；治疗癫痫、脑内给药、植入深部脑刺激器等医疗设备
text_en: Your doctor may recommend a craniotomy to remove abnormal brain tissue such as a brain tumor, a sample of tissue by biopsy, a blood clot called a hematoma, excess cerebrospinal fluid, or pus from an infection called an abscess. A craniotomy may also be done to relieve brain swelling, stop bleeding called a hemorrhage, repair abnormal blood vessels, repair skull fractures, or repair damaged meninges. Finally, a craniotomy may also be done to treat brain conditions such as epilepsy, deliver medication to your brain, or implant a medical device such as a deep brain stimulator.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 手术准备：以切除脑肿瘤为例，患者接受全身麻醉，在肿瘤位置剃除头皮毛发
text_en: The most common reason for a craniotomy is to remove a brain tumor. For your procedure, you will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free. The skin on your scalp will be shaved over the location of the tumor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 开颅术手术步骤：切开头皮，在颅骨上钻孔并连接形成骨瓣后取下，切开硬脑膜暴露脑组织，切除肿瘤，缝合硬脑膜，用金属板和螺钉将骨瓣复位固定
text_en: To begin, your surgeon will make an incision in your scalp. One or more small holes will be made in your skull. Then your surgeon will connect the holes to create a circular piece of bone called a bone flap and remove it. Your surgeon will make an incision in the duromotor to expose your brain. Then your tumor will be removed. The incision in the duromotor will be closed with sutures. Your surgeon will reattach the bone flap with metal plates and sprues that will remain in your skull for life.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 去骨瓣减压术（craniectomy）：当脑肿胀严重或颅骨感染时，术中不立即回置骨瓣，待肿胀消退后再行回置；无论哪种术式，最后用订书钉缝合头皮切口并包扎
text_en: You may have a variation on a craniotomy called a craniectomy if your brain is very swollen or your skull is infected. Your surgeon may delay reattaching the bone flap until a later procedure to allow time for the swelling to go down. At the end of either a craniotomy or a craniectomy, your scalp incision will be closed with staples. And your head will be wrapped in bandages.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 颅骨成形术（cranioplasty）：去骨瓣减压术后待肿胀消退，择期行颅骨成形术，用金属板和螺钉重新固定骨瓣并缝合皮肤切口
text_en: If you had a craniectomy, you will have another procedure called a cranioplasty at a later date after the swelling goes down. At that time, your surgeon will reattach your bone flap with metal plates and sprues. Then close the skin incision with staples.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 急性冠脉综合征的定义：由心肌供血阻塞引起的一组疾病，属医学急症，可能正在发生心肌梗死（心肌开始坏死）。
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, you have one or more conditions caused by a blockage of blood flow to your heart muscle. This is a medical emergency because you may be having a heart attack, a condition in which your heart muscle begins to die.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 心脏正常解剖与供血机制：心脏作为泵血器官，左心室收缩将富氧血液经主动脉泵出，冠状动脉从主动脉分支为心肌提供氧气和营养。
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs. The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle. When your left ventricle contracts, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through a large artery called the aorta. Connected to your aorta are small arteries called coronary arteries. Blood flows from your aorta through the coronary arteries to supply your heart muscle with oxygen and nutrients.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 急性冠脉综合征的常见病因——动脉粥样硬化：脂肪斑块在冠状动脉内壁积聚使管腔狭窄，斑块破裂可形成血栓阻塞动脉，血栓是冠脉阻塞最常见的原因。
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, blood flow through your coronary arteries is severely reduced or completely blocked. One possible cause of reduced blood flow is atherosclerosis. In this condition, a build-up of a fatty substance called plaque can narrow your coronary If this plaque ruptures, a blood clot can form and block the artery. A blood clot is the most common cause of coronary artery blockage.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 其他较少见病因：冠状动脉痉挛（药物、吸烟、寒冷、极端情绪等诱因导致冠脉突然收缩）和冠状动脉夹层（冠脉内壁分离阻断血流）。
text_en: Other less common causes of reduced blood flow include coronary artery spasm or dissection. In a coronary artery spasm, triggers such as drugs, smoking, cold weather, and extreme stress or emotions can cause a temporary and sudden tightening of a coronary artery. During a coronary artery dissection, the inside wall of one of your coronary arteries separates which can block blood flow.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 冠脉阻塞的后果——心肌梗死：无论何种原因，冠脉阻塞使对应区域心肌缺氧缺营养并开始坏死，即心脏病发作（心肌梗死/MI）。
text_en: Regardless of the cause, a blockage in either coronary artery prevents the oxygen and nutrients in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery. As a result, heart muscle in that area starts to die. Death of part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack. It's also known as a myocardial infarction or MI.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 急性冠脉综合征的症状：胸部疼痛、紧缩或烧灼感（心绞痛），可放射至上腹部、肩部、手臂、颈部和下颌；静息或轻度活动时发作的不稳定型心绞痛是ACS的主要症状；其他症状包括呼吸困难、头晕、恶心和出汗。
text_en: A blocked coronary artery may also cause you to feel sudden pain, discomfort, tightening, or a burning sensation in your chest called angina. This pain may extend to your upper abdomen, shoulders, arms, neck, and lower jaw. If you have angina when you're at rest or frequent angina that prevents even moderate physical activity, you have unstable angina which is the main symptom of acute coronary Other symptoms of acute coronary syndrome include shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea, and sweating.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 药物治疗：氧疗增加血氧、阿司匹林等抗凝药预防血栓、溶栓药溶解已有血栓、硝酸甘油和吗啡扩张冠脉并缓解心绞痛、β受体阻滞剂减慢心率降低心肌耗氧。
text_en: If you've had a heart attack or have other types of acute coronary syndrome, your doctor may prescribe oxygen therapy to get more oxygen into your blood. You may take aspirin or other prescription blood thinner drugs to prevent blood clots. Thrombolytics, also known as clot buster drugs, may be used to break up any existing blood Cards such as nitroglycerin and morphine will relax your coronary arteries and relieve the pain of angina. You may also receive drugs called beta blockers that slow down your heart and reduce its need for oxygen.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 手术治疗：冠脉血管成形术（球囊扩张阻塞的冠脉并可植入支架保持通畅）和冠状动脉旁路移植术（CABG，用静脉或人工移植材料绕过阻塞区域）。
text_en: Your doctor may also recommend immediate surgical procedures such as coronary angioplasty, in which a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary artery to open it. After inflating, the balloon catheter may leave behind a mesh-like device called a stent to hold your artery open. Or you may have a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage. Cigilage is a surgical procedure in which the blocked areas of the coronary arteries are bypassed with veins or artificial graft material.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 精索静脉曲张的定义：阴囊内一组肿胀的静脉血管
text_en: A varicoseal is a group of swollen blood vessels called veins in a man's scrotum.
